Issuances thereof should be recorded as they take place using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) which shall be submitted by the General Service Officer or Local Treasurer as the case maybe.

It was recommended that the agency strictly observe the proper accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units in the recording of transactions relative to monetization of leave credits.

It was recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 100 and 272 which govern the disposition of the Special Education Fund and refrain from spending government funds for religious or private purposes in compliance with the provisions of Section 335 of the Local Government Code.

It was recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact her collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid the risk of loss or improper utilization of government funds.

It was reiterated that the procurement of goods which are similar in nature be consolidated and procured through public bidding. The Bids and Awards Committee, who has the responsibility for ensuring that the procuring entity abides by the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its IRR, should strictly adhere with all provisions of law, rules and regulations pertaining to procurements to protect at all times the interest of the local government unit.

It was recommended that management require the concerned personnel to submit the copies of perfected contracts to the Audit Team within five working days as  prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No.  2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order to allow the Audit Team to perform effective review and generate timely and relevant audit results. Administrative  disciplinary action as provided in Section 4 of the same circular against the concerned employee be imposed upon unjustified failure of compliance.

It was recommended that management furnish the Office of the Auditor the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site in compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-04 and in order for us to validate the infrastructure projects on time.

It was reiterated that the concerned personnel exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts.  This is to avoid undue delays that preclude the timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

It was recommended that the local government unit refrain from allowing deductions from salaries, emoluments or other benefits accruing to their employees which shall reduce the employee’s monthly net take home pay to an amount lower than Three Thousand Pesos (P3,000) in compliance with Section 48 of Republic Act No. 10651 also known as General Appropriation Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2015.

It was recommended that local government unit prohibit disposing wastes through incineration and require the establishment of MRF in every barangay or cluster barangays in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program to protect the constituents against human health and environment hazards.

These will provide additional funds to support the projected expenditure of the LGU which may be beneficial to its constituents.

be submitted to oversight agencies on due date as required under Item 7 – C6 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint  Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014 – 001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the General Service Office  to prepare an inventory report of all  insurable properties of the municipality and submit to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS for proper processing of the insurance coverage of the all insurable properties  of the municipality so that in case of loss, destruction and/or damages,  the cost of these assets will be recovered.
